categories.title: Introduction
categories.blurb: The world you inherit, and the fire you keep.

entries.body: Once the Sacred Flame was tended whole in the Secret Tower, and the world was whole with it. Then its keepers vanished, the Tower went dark — and the world began, slowly, to forget. Magic thins, runes slip from memory, and the great powers of the old age have dwindled to a scatter of guttering hearths in the dark.

You are the appointed Archon of Runehold — though who named you, and why, is itself half-forgotten. As the Tower's enemies gathered, the coven that kept it broke the Flame into Embers and scattered them beyond reach. One was carried to your hearth-rune: the coven's heart, and its last thread to the old power. Let it die, and Runehold dies with it.

Beyond your walls the seven Colour Cults circle — each hoarding what magic remains, and no two of them allies for long. Win one to your side and you make an enemy of another. There is no pleasing all seven.

Your Wizards will counsel you, and quarrel. Famine, feud, and worse are coming. Lead them through it — and, if the old stories are true, seek the other Embers before the dark finds them first. Keep the flame, or be forgotten.

categories.title: The Pressures
categories.blurb: Seven meters and your standing with the Cults. Every scene reads and writes them.

entries.body: Your spellcasting reserve. Powers workings, quests, and defenses. Regenerates slowly, spent in bursts.
Food for the coven in a broken world. Famine is a constant low threat; a coven cannot eat runes.
The mercantile axis, ruled by the Gilded Reach and the Red Cult. Buys what magic and farming cannot — and ties you to aristocrat politics.
Runes and memory: the signature meter. Unlocks great workings, but DECAYS every season — 'The Forgetting' made mechanical. Maintain libraries, train apprentices, or watch power slip away.
The coven's morale — whether your Wizards believe you can lead them through the dark. Driven by Circle relationships and consistency with your values.

The cosmic meter. Drawing on the Sacred Flame is the highest reward and the highest risk: salvation, transformation, or undesirable loss. VEILED by default — you feel its pull through what your choices cost, but the running number is hidden. Reveal it under Options → Display.

The doom track. The cataclysm is not finished — Imps, stirring Beasts, and the slow Forgetting advance it. Reaches its limit and Runehold is consumed. VEILED by default — read its mood on the Map (quiet → stirring → rising → dire) rather than a number; reveal the exact meter under Options → Display.

categories.title: The Seven Color Cults
categories.blurb: Seven independent reputations. Acting for one often costs another.

entries.body: the Gilded Reach — greedy aristocrats and the Stock Sanctuary. The mercantile and coin power of the Runiverse.
The Bastion — the largest libraries and the Forgotten Athenaeum. The lore and knowledge power.
The chroniclers have not yet fixed this cult's character.

categories.title: Powers & Perils
categories.blurb: The forces your saga is written against.

entries.body: Once tended whole in the Secret Tower, the greatest power and the greatest loss in the world. When the Tower's keepers vanished and its enemies gathered, the coven that kept it broke the Flame into EMBERS and scattered them to hidden hearths rather than let it be seized. Draw on an Ember and you may work great magic — but Wizards who step too far into it become Forgotten Souls (roughly one in ten, something undesirable).

The shards of the Sacred Flame, carried out of the Secret Tower and scattered so no enemy could take the whole. Each is the burning heart of a hearth like Runehold. How many survived, and where, is unknown — kept, lost, gone cold, or in the wrong hands. Reuniting the Embers is the long road back to re-lighting the Tower. You hold one; there are others to find.

The world is literally forgetting itself — magic thins, runes slip from memory, and once-simple workings unravel. In game terms Lore decays every season unless held. Tomes and Stories the coven recovers point to a single cause: the Forgetting began when the Flame was broken. What that truly means, the wise are only starting to grasp.
The cataclysm that tore the world apart is not finished. It is the long game beneath all the others — the doom that takes Runehold if neglected.

Your advisor ring is rolled fresh each campaign — Wizards, each with a school (colour), a class, a rank, an age, four competence stats (Power, Wisdom, Guile, Courage) and a temperament (Boldness, Piety, Temper). They grow a year older each year — their portrait ages with them. On a scene, whoever fits the moment speaks — and they disagree. Competence decides what they can pull off; temperament decides what they urge. The second-in-command casts your workings by default.

Every Wizard practises one of the seven colour schools (Red, Yellow, Brown, Green, Blue, Purple, White) and holds one of nine classes: Magus, Sorcerer, Druid, Necromancer, Pyromancer, Enchanter, Charmer, Chaos Mage, Ghost Eater. School is the flavour of the magic; class is what they do best. Who stands in your Circle shapes what workings the coven can attempt.

Spells the coven casts on its own initiative, on the Workings screen. Each belongs to a colour school and tests one competence (Power, Wisdom, Guile, or Courage) against a difficulty. You choose the caster — the second-in-command casts by default. A caster whose school matches the working, backed by high mastery, casts at far better odds; on a win the working takes full effect, on a loss a weaker one. Nothing is ever certain.

How deeply the coven commands each of the seven schools (0–100), separate from your standing with the cults. Opening mastery reflects who sits in your Circle — the schools your Wizards practise start strong, the rest near nothing. Casting a working of a school practises it, nudging that mastery up. Higher mastery raises the odds on every working of that colour.
Wizards transformed by the Flame. When a Circle member is taken, the loss is permanent — they leave the Circle but persist in the world as a Soul you may later meet, bargain with, or mourn.

Fragments of the old world the coven recovers — a burned tome, a survivor's account, a cautionary tale of some great Wizard who reached for an epic working and failed catastrophically as the memory drained out from under them. Each fills in the Codex and, piece by piece, the truth of the Flame and the Embers. The Blue Bastion and its Forgotten Athenaeum chase them hardest.

Your coven seat, and one of the scattered hearths — a few dozen souls keeping an EMBER of the Sacred Flame alive in a guttering hearth-rune. It is the coven's heart and its last thread to the old power: let it die and Runehold dies with it. Grow it from a hearth to a power, or dwindle into a Forgotten hold.
